UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile and non-authentication related settings within the PocketFlow application. It links to an external authentication system (e.g., Firebase).

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

firebaseUid.description: The unique identifier provided by Firebase Authentication for the associated user account. This links the application's internal user data to the external authentication system. (Relationship: FirebaseUser 1:1 UserProfile)

email.description: The user's email address, primarily for display and communication within the app. Should be sourced from the authentication system.

displayName.description: The user's preferred name for display within the application.

currencyCode.description: The ISO 4217 currency code (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR') preferred by the user for financial data display and calculations.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was created in the application's database.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was last updated.

Category.title: Category
Category.description: Defines custom categories for financial transactions (expenses and incomes), allowing users to organize their spending and earnings.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Category entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile this category belongs to. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Category)

name.description: The descriptive name of the category (e.g., 'Groceries', 'Salary', 'Rent').

type.description: The type of transaction this category applies to, either 'expense' or 'income'.

iconName.description: Optional name of an icon associated with the category for visual representation in the UI.

colorCode.description: Optional hexadecimal color code for the category for UI representation (e.g., '#FF0000').

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the category was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the category was last updated.

Transaction.title: Transaction
Transaction.description: Records individual financial transactions, including both expenses and incomes, linked to a user and a specific category.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Transaction entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who recorded this transaction. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Transaction)

categoryId.description: Reference to the Category this transaction belongs to. (Relationship: Category 1:N Transaction)

amount.description: The monetary value of the transaction. Positive for income, negative for expense (or handled by 'type' field).

type.description: The type of the transaction, either 'expense' or 'income'.

description.description: A brief description or merchant name for the transaction.

date.description: The date when the transaction occurred.

isRecurrent.description: Indicates whether this transaction is part of a recurring series (e.g., monthly rent, salary).

notes.description: Optional longer notes or additional details about the transaction.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the transaction record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the transaction record was last updated.

Budget.title: Budget
Budget.description: Defines monthly spending limits for specific categories, allowing users to track their progress against financial plans.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Budget entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who set this budget. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Budget)

categoryId.description: Reference to the Category this budget applies to. (Relationship: Category 1:N Budget)

month.description: The month (YYYY-MM format) for which this budget applies.

limitAmount.description: The maximum monetary amount budgeted for the category for the specified month.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the budget was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the budget was last updated.

SpendingGoal.title: SpendingGoal
SpendingGoal.description: Allows users to set and monitor financial goals, such as saving for a specific item, debt reduction, or investment targets.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SpendingGoal entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who set this spending goal. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N SpendingGoal)

name.description: A descriptive name for the spending goal (e.g., 'Vacation Fund', 'New Laptop', 'Emergency Savings').

targetAmount.description: The total monetary amount required to achieve the goal.

currentAmount.description: The current monetary amount saved or contributed towards the goal.

targetDate.description: The desired date by which the goal is intended to be achieved.

startDate.description: The date when the spending goal was initiated.

isCompleted.description: Indicates whether the spending goal has been successfully met.

notes.description: Optional longer notes or additional context about the spending goal.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the spending goal was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the spending goal was last updated.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. The `{userId}` corresponds directly to the Firebase Authentication UID of the logged-in user. This path establishes ownership for all subcollections, and the document itself includes `firebaseUid` for explicit linking to Auth. Includes denormalized 'firebaseUid' and 'id' fields that match the path 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, corresponding to their Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Stores custom financial categories defined by each user. Each category document contains the `userId` field matching the parent `userId` in the path, ensuring authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this category.

params.name: categoryId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific category.

definition.description: Records individual financial transactions (expenses/income) for each user. Each transaction document contains the `userId` field matching the parent `userId` in the path for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who recorded this transaction.

params.name: transactionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific transaction.

definition.description: Stores monthly budget limits set by users for specific categories. Each budget document contains the `userId` field matching the parent `userId` in the path for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who set this budget.

params.name: budgetId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific budget.

definition.description: Allows users to define and track progress towards financial goals. Each spending goal document contains the `userId` field matching the parent `userId` in the path for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who set this spending goal.

params.name: spendingGoalId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific spending goal.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for PocketFlow is designed to be highly secure, scalable, and debuggable, adhering strictly to the core design principles and strategy mandates. The primary strategy revolves around **Path-Based Ownership** and **Authorization Independence via Denormalization**.

**Authorization Independence:** All user-specific data (UserProfile, Category, Transaction, Budget, SpendingGoal) is nested under a top-level `/users/{userId}` collection. The `{userId}` wildcard in the path directly corresponds to the `request.auth.uid` of the authenticated user. Crucially, every document in these subcollections (e.g., `/users/{userId}/categories/{categoryId}`) explicitly includes a `userId` field. This denormalization means that security rules do not need to perform `get()` operations on parent documents to verify ownership or access rights. Instead, rules can simply compare `request.auth.uid` with the `{userId}` in the path and the `userId` field within the document itself, ensuring atomic operations (transactions, batch writes) are straightforward and secure. For example, a rule for categories could be `allow read, write: if request.auth.uid == request.resource.data.userId && request.auth.uid == userId;` (where `userId` is the path wildcard).

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** By enforcing this hierarchical, user-owned structure, the design inherently supports Query-Ability and Predictability (QAPs). When a user queries a collection like `/users/{request.auth.uid}/transactions`, Firestore's security rules can easily and efficiently filter access, ensuring that `list` operations only return data owned by the authenticated user. The rules simply verify that the `userId` in the path matches `request.auth.uid`, making it impossible for a user to inadvertently or maliciously query another user's data. This structural segregation (`Structural Segregation` mandate) means that all documents within a given user's subcollections (`/users/{userId}/categories`, `/users/{userId}/transactions`, etc.) share the exact same security posture: only the owner can access them. This simplifies security rules immensely and avoids complex, performance-intensive filtering logic within the rules themselves.

**Access Modeling:** The design consistently uses `Path-Based Ownership` for all private user data, following the mandate to `Use Hierarchical Paths for User-Owned Data`. There are no collaborative entities or global roles in the initial scope, so `Membership Maps` and `Global Roles` patterns are not applied here. The `id` field of each entity is designed to be the document ID within its respective collection, with `firebaseUid` in `UserProfile` serving as the definitive link to Firebase Authentication and the document ID for the `UserProfile` itself.
